heading: Exercise 1
text: What is sound and how is it produced

heading: Exercise 2
text:
Describe with the help of a diagram, how compressions and
rarefactions are produced in air near a source of sound.

heading: Exercise 3
text: Why is sound wave called a longitudinal wave

heading: Exercise 4
text:
Which characteristic of the sound helps you to identify your
friend by his voice while sitting with others in a dark room

heading: Exercise 5
text:
Flash and thunder are produced simultaneously. But
thunder is heard a few seconds after the flash is seen, why

heading: Exercise 6
text:
A person has a hearing range from 20 Hz to 20 kHz. What
are the typical wavelengths of sound waves in air
corresponding to these two frequencies Take the speed of
sound in air as 344 m s–1.

heading: Exercise 7
text:
Two children are at opposite ends of an aluminium rod. One
strikes the end of the rod with a stone. Find the ratio of
times taken by the sound wave in air and in aluminium to
reach the second child.

heading: Exercise 8
text:
The frequency of a source of sound is 100 Hz. How many
times does it vibrate in a minute

heading: Exercise 9
text:
Does sound follow the same laws of reflection as light does
Explain.

heading: Exercise 10
text:
When a sound is reflected from a distant object, an echo is
produced. Let the distance between the reflecting surface
and the source of sound production remains the same. Do
you hear echo sound on a hotter day

heading: Exercise 11
text: Give two practical applications of reflection of sound waves.

heading: Exercise 12
text:
A stone is dropped from the top of a tower 500 m high into a
pond of water at the base of the tower. When is the splash
heard at the top Given, g 10 m s–2 and speed of sound
340 m s–1.

heading: Exercise 13
text:
A sound wave travels at a speed of 339 m s–1. If its wavelength
is 1.5 cm, what is the frequency of the wave Will it be
audible

heading: Exercise 14
text: What is reverberation How can it be reduced

heading: Exercise 15
text: What is loudness of sound What factors does it depend on

heading: Exercise 16
text: How is ultrasound used for cleaning

heading: Exercise 17
text:
Explain how defects in a metal block can be detected using
ultrasound.
